#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Check if NetworkManager is installed
if ! command -v nmcli >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "NetworkManager is not installed, skipping remediation."
    exit 0
fi

# Enable nullglob for safe globbing
shopt -s nullglob

# Directory for drop-ins
CONF_DIR="/etc/NetworkManager/conf.d"
MAIN_CONF="/etc/NetworkManager/NetworkManager.conf"

# Function to ensure dns=strict in a file
ensure_dns_strict() {
    local f="$1"
    # Replace any existing dns line
    sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*dns=[^[:space:]]*/dns=strict/I' "$f"
    # If still no dns line, add after [main] if present
    if ! grep -qiE '^[[:space:]]*dns=' "$f"; then
        if grep -qiE '^[[:space:]]*\[main\]' "$f"; then
            sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*\[main\]/ a\dns=strict' "$f"
        fi
    fi
}

# Process main config
if [ -f "$MAIN_CONF" ]; then
    ensure_dns_strict "$MAIN_CONF"
else
    # If main config doesn't exist, we might create it later if needed
    true
fi

# Process drop-in files
for f in "$CONF_DIR"/*.conf; do
    if [ -f "$f" ]; then
        ensure_dns_strict "$f"
    fi
done

# Check if any config has dns=strict
if ! grep -rqiE '^[[:space:]]*dns=strict' "$MAIN_CONF $CONF_DIR" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Create the drop-in directory if it doesn't exist
    mkdir -p "$CONF_DIR"
    # Create a new drop-in file with dns=strict
    echo -e "[main]\ndns=strict" > "$CONF_DIR/99-dns-strict.conf"
fi
